message.content:
Adversarially verify this finding about a design doc (the doc is at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-jleechan-projects-worktree-runner/8ca589dd-8422-44b1-a543-52bd7b71f14b/scratchpad/gistfile1.txt; the repo is /home/jleechan/projects/worktree_runner; you may use WebSearch/WebFetch and read files). Try to REFUTE it. Finding from the "architecture" reviewer, severity critical:
TITLE: Responsibility boundary with gha-outrunner is never specified
DETAIL: The entire design delegates execution to gha-outrunner ('Start gha-outrunner with correct flags/backend', src/runner.rs), but never states which responsibilities outrunner owns versus the wrapper: registration tokens? image pulls? post-job VM destruction? restart on failure? status reporting? Every other gap in this review is either a real gap or outrunner's job — and the design doesn't say which. A wrapper design must enumerate the wrapped tool's contract (config schema it accepts, exit/restart behavior, what it cleans up) or the wrapper's own scope is undefined. The generated YAML is also invented ad-hoc (format! strings) with no reference to outrunner's actual schema, no schema version, and no validation that outrunner accepts it.
RECOMMENDATION: Add a 'Contract with gha-outrunner' section: exact config schema (with a version field), which lifecycle phases outrunner owns, its failure/restart semantics, and how the wrapper observes it (exit codes, status API, log format). Every gap in this review should be explicitly assigned to one side of the boundary.
Is the finding factually correct and does the recommendation make sense? If the factual basis is wrong or the severity is inflated, refute it. Default to refuted=true only if you have concrete contrary evidence or the claim is unsupported after checking.

content.content:
Web search results for query: "gha-outrunner GitHub Actions runner"

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner: Ephemeral VM and container runners for GitHub Actions — no Kubernetes required","url":"https://github.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner"},{"title":"Get started with Actions Runner Controller - GitHub Docs","url":"https://docs.github.com/en/actions/tutorials/use-actions-runner-controller/get-started"},{"title":"GitHub - rust-lang/gha-runner: Custom fork of the GitHub Actions runner","url":"https://github.com/rust-lang/gha-runner"},{"title":"GitHub - chaddyc/gha-runner: GitHub Actions Runner Docker Container · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/chaddyc/gha-runner"},{"title":"Deploying runner scale sets with Actions Runner Controller - GitHub Docs","url":"https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/manage-runners/use-actions-runner-controller/deploy-runner-scale-sets"},{"title":"Actions Runner Controller - GitHub Docs","url":"https://docs.github.com/en/actions/concepts/runners/actions-runner-controller"},{"title":"GitHub - omsf/gha-runner: A simple GitHub Action for creating cloud-based self-hosted runners. · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/omsf/gha-runner"},{"title":"GitHub - PasseiDireto/gh-runner: Self Hosted GitHub Actions Runner · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/PasseiDireto/gh-runner"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about gha-outrunner:

## gha-outrunner Overview

gha-outrunner provides ephemeral GitHub Actions runners without requiring Kubernetes. It's a project created by Netwind HQ that offers an alternative to GitHub's Actions Runner Controller (ARC).

## Key Features

Outrunner provisions fresh Docker containers or VMs for each GitHub Actions job, then destroys them when the job completes. It uses GitHub's scaleset API to register as an autoscaling runner group.

## Advantages Over ARC

While GitHub's Actions Runner Controller (ARC) requires Kubernetes, if you're running on bare metal or a simple VPS, you shouldn't need a cluster just to get ephemeral runners. Outrunner gives you the same isolation guarantees with Docker, libvirt, or Tart without any additional orchestrator needed.

## Supported Backends

The tool supports multiple provisioning options:
- Container per job for fastest startup
- KVM VM from qcow2 golden image with CoW overlays using QEMU Guest Agent for command execution
- VM clone per job using Tart guest agent for command execution

The project is hosted on GitHub at https://github.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

input.prompt: Describe the configuration file format/schema this tool accepts (file name, YAML structure, top-level keys, runner/backend config fields, resource limit fields), how it's invoked (CLI flags), and what lifecycle responsibilities it owns (registration tokens/scaleset registration, image pulls, VM destruction after job, restart on failure, status reporting). Quote relevant config examples.

content.content:
# gha-outrunner Configuration and Lifecycle

## Configuration File Format

The page references a "Configuration reference" document but doesn't display its contents. Based on the repository structure, configuration appears to use YAML format with a `config.go` file handling parsing.

## Invocation

The tool is invoked via CLI with a Makefile and command structure located in `cmd/outrunner`. Specific CLI flags aren't detailed in the provided content, though the documentation notes a "CLI reference" exists.

## Core Responsibilities

**Registration & Scaleset Integration:**
The tool "uses GitHub's scaleset API to register as an autoscaling runner group," enabling dynamic runner provisioning.

**Lifecycle Management:**
The system "provisions fresh Docker containers or VMs for each GitHub Actions job, then destroys them when the job completes," ensuring ephemeral isolation.

**Provisioner Options:**
Three backends handle different environments:
- Docker (Linux containers, fastest startup)
- libvirt (KVM VMs with "CoW overlays" and guest agent execution)
- Tart (macOS/ARM64 VMs with "guest agent for command execution")

**Image Requirements:**
Documentation specifies what "each backend expects" for runner images, with custom image guides for Docker, Windows, and Tart platforms.

The project emphasizes zero Kubernetes overhead while maintaining "the same isolation guarantees" as controller-based alternatives.

content.content:
# gha-outrunner Documentation Structure

Based on the GitHub repository page, the **docs** directory contains the following documentation sections:

## Directory Structure

1. **explanation/** - Conceptual documentation
2. **howto/** - How-to guides and tutorials
3. **reference/** - API and configuration reference
4. **setup/** - Installation and setup instructions
5. **tutorial/** - Step-by-step tutorials

## Additional Resources

- **arch.png** - Architecture diagram
- **info.png** - Information graphic

## Note on Configuration Details

The webpage content provided does not display the actual documentation files or YAML schema details. To access the specific configuration reference, runner pool definitions, or resource limit fields, you would need to navigate into the **reference/** directory or view the actual configuration files in the repository's source code.

For detailed schema information, I recommend checking the `reference/` subdirectory or the project's main README file in the repository root.

content.content:

The directory contains the following files:

1. **cli.md** - Command-line interface documentation
2. **configuration.md** - Configuration reference guide
3. **image-requirements.md** - Requirements for container images

These reference documents appear to provide technical specifications and guidance for the gha-outrunner project.

input.prompt: Reproduce the YAML config schema: the config file name, top-level keys, how runner pools/scale sets are defined, backend selection fields, resource limit fields (memory/cpus/count), and any version field. Quote an example config verbatim.

content.content:
# outrunner Configuration Schema

**Config File:** `/etc/outrunner/config.yml` (overridable via `--config` flag)

## Top-Level Keys

- `url` – Global repository/organization URL
- `token_file` – Path to GitHub PAT file
- `runners` – Map of scale set definitions

## Runner Pool Definition

Runner scale sets are defined as map entries under `runners.<scale-set-name>`, where the key becomes the GitHub scale set name. Each runner must specify exactly one backend: `docker`, `libvirt`, or `tart`.

## Backend Selection

- **`docker`** – Container-based runners
- **`libvirt`** – KVM/QEMU virtual machines
- **`tart`** – Tart VM clones (macOS/Linux)

## Resource Limit Fields

| Field | Applies To | Purpose |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| `max_runners` | All backends | Concurrent runner limit (defaults to `--max-runners` flag) |
| `cpus` | libvirt, tart | vCPU allocation |
| `memory` | libvirt, tart | Memory in MiB |

No version field exists in this schema.

## Example Configuration

```yaml
url: https://github.com/myorg
token_file: /etc/outrunner/token

runners:
  linux:
    labels: [self-hosted, linux]
    max_runners: 4
    docker:
      image: outrunner-runner:latest
      mounts:
        - source: /var/cache/vcpkg
          target: /opt/vcpkg-cache
```

input.reasoning:
I attempted to refute the finding and instead found additional confirming evidence.

1. The doc genuinely never specifies the boundary. Read in full (292 lines, /tmp/.../scratchpad/gistfile1.txt): the only statements about gha-outrunner are "Start gha-outrunner with correct flags/backend" (line 33), the module table entry "src/runner.rs — Start/stop/status of gha-outrunner" (line 73-74), and "Ensure prerequisites (gha-outrunner binary...)". There is no section on registration tokens, GitHub auth, image management, post-job cleanup, restart/failure semantics, exit codes, or status observation. The closest counter-evidence is the module table (which defines the wrapper's modules) and the core principle "Use GitHub Scale Sets + ephemeral runners by default" — but neither assigns that behavior to either side, so the finding stands.

2. gha-outrunner is a real tool (github.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner) and its actual documented config schema (docs/reference/configuration.md) contradicts the doc's generated YAML on nearly every field, which makes the "invented ad-hoc" claim not just correct but understated. Real schema: top-level `url` and `token_file` are how registration happens (both ABSENT from the generated config — the runner could not register at all); backends are specified as a map key (`docker:`/`libvirt:`/`tart:` block), not a `backend:` scalar field as the doc generates; concurrency is `max_runners`, not `count`; `cpus`/`memory` (MiB) live inside the libvirt/tart block, not a `resources:` block with "4G"; docker requires an `image` field (missing); no documented `pids_limit` or docker memory/cpus resource block. The doc's config.rs format! strings would produce a config outrunner rejects or cannot use.

3. The doc also invents a `DockerSysbox` backend variant that has no counterpart in outrunner's documented backends (docker/libvirt/tart only), and outrunner itself owns exactly the lifecycle phases the finding lists (scaleset registration, per-job provision/destroy, image expectations per its docs) — none of which the design acknowledges, and one of which ("Scale Sets + ephemeral") the design implicitly claims as its own principle.

Severity "critical" is not inflated: the wrapper's entire purpose is generating config for and driving outrunner, and the demonstrated schema divergence plus total absence of auth (url/token_file) means the design as written produces a non-functional system. The recommendation (add a "Contract with gha-outrunner" section, enumerate lifecycle ownership, validate against the real schema) is directly actionable. One minor quibble: the recommendation asks for a config "version field," but outrunner's real schema has no version field, so that sub-point should be "pin/reference the outrunner version and its documented schema" rather than inventing a version field outrunner won't read — this does not change the verdict. Sources: https://github.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner and https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner/main/docs/reference/configuration.md.
input.corrected_claim: Finding is correct as stated; only refinement: instead of demanding a schema "version field" in the generated YAML (gha-outrunner's real schema has none), the contract section should pin the targeted gha-outrunner release and mirror its documented schema (top-level url/token_file, backend-name map keys, max_runners, backend-scoped cpus/memory) — the doc's generated YAML currently matches none of these.
